title: What to know about high blood pressure before using medicinal herbs
intro: Herbal teas, such as hibiscus, holy basil, and mistletoe, are scientifically proven remedies for high blood pressure. Hibiscus tea, rich in antioxidants, effectively lowers systolic blood pressure. Holy basil, a renowned adaptogen, reduces blood pressure by managing stress and anxiety. Mistletoe tea, with its anti-hypertensive and anti-inflammatory properties, has been used for centuries to manage hypertension. Including these teas in your daily routine can significantly improve high blood pressure symptoms.
definition: High blood pressure, also known as hypertension, is a medical condition characterized by consistently elevated force of blood against artery walls. This situation occurs when the heart pumps blood with greater power than normal, causing the blood to exert excessive pressure on artery walls. If left uncontrolled, high blood pressure can lead to severe health complications, such as heart disease, stroke, and kidney failure. Given these risks, maintaining healthy blood pressure is vital for safeguarding long-term well-being.
